rawCommits.subject: style(update-banner): use black background for ready variant
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(update-banner): use black background for ready variant

rawCommits.subject: fix(skills): block delete while installed elsewhere and hide stale catalog entries
rawCommits.body:
The "Delete skill" flow in the agent sidekick had two gaps that let
state drift between agents:

1. Deleting a user-authored skill from one agent removed the SKILL.md
   file on disk but left every other agent's installation record
   pointing at a now-missing file. The UI showed the cascade warning
   but only uninstalled from the current agent before firing the
   delete, so CEO would still list the skill as installed after
   Agent02 deleted it.
2. The harness catalog (GET api/skills) is in-memory and only
   reconciles on rescan, so a just-deleted skill kept reappearing
   under "Available" until the harness caught up.

Server-side delete_my_skill now enumerates local agents, asks the
harness which skills each one has installed, and refuses with 409
when the skill is still live anywhere -- returning the offending
{agent_id, name} list so the UI can tell the user which agents to
clean up first. The list_skills proxy drops any catalog entry whose
~/.aura/skills/<name>/SKILL.md is gone, so the filesystem stays the
source of truth for what shows under "Available".

The SkillsTab delete flow drops the pre-emptive current-agent
uninstall (it was masking the real state) and renders the 409
blocker list inline in the confirm modal with the Delete button
disabled until every blocking agent has uninstalled.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: block delete while installed elsewhere and hide stale catalog entries

rawCommits.subject: refactor(agent-tools): unify tool registry with Surface-declared session inclusion
rawCommits.body:
Replace the fragmented "tier-1 / tier-2" + CEO-preset short-circuit model
with a single capability-aware registry filtered at session-open time.

Every tool now declares `fn surface() -> Surface` (`Always` or
`OnDemand`), and `AgentPermissions::ceo_preset()` carries the new
`Capability::ReadAllProjects` / `WriteAllProjects` wildcards so CEO
and non-CEO bundles flow through exactly the same
`permissions_satisfy_requirements` + `build_session_tool_names`
pipeline. This fixes the reported CEO bug where `list_agents`,
`send_to_agent`, and `list_agent_instances` were missing from the
live session: `list_agent_instances` is now `Surface::Always` and
its `ReadProjectFromArg` requirement is satisfied via
`ReadAllProjects`.

Also invalidate the agent's direct chat session and the dispatcher
`permissions_cache` entry in `update_agent`, so toggling capabilities
in the UI takes effect on the next chat turn without a server restart.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: unify tool registry with Surface-declared session inclusion

rawCommits.subject: feat(settings): add upgrade control under version
rawCommits.body:
Add an update status block beneath the Settings 'Version' row so desktop
users can see whether they are on the latest version, check for updates,
install available updates, and observe download/install/failure states.
Falls back to a 'managed by the server' message on the web where the
native updater is unavailable.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add upgrade control under version

rawCommits.subject: fix(permissions): invalidate every session owned by an agent on caps change
rawCommits.body:
The previous invalidation only dropped the `agent:{id}` direct-chat
session. Regular agents in a project context actually chat through
`instance:{agent_instance_id}` sessions, whose stale `installed_tools`
list survived the permissions update and caused the reported
"I enabled ReadAgent but `list_agents` still isn't available" bug.

Tag each `ChatSession` with its owning agent id at open time
(populated from `SessionConfig::agent_id`, which already carries the
canonical agent id for both direct and instance flows) so
`update_agent` can sweep the registry and drop every session owned by
the agent whose capabilities just changed — direct and instance alike.
The next chat turn cold-starts and re-seeds `installed_tools` through
the unified capability-aware `build_session_tools` filter.

Also restyle the capability toggles in `PermissionsTab` with a bordered
black track and high-contrast knob so the on/off state is visible
against the dark panel background.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: invalidate every session owned by an agent on caps change

rawCommits.subject: fix(permissions): read fresh parent caps per instance chat turn, guard PUT echo
rawCommits.body:
Two gaps kept regular-agent capability toggles from taking effect
after Save:

1. Project-instance chats used `instance.permissions`, a snapshot
   captured when the instance was first bound to its project. Toggling
   a capability on the agent template updated `Agent.permissions` but
   never touched the instance snapshot, so every project-bound chat
   kept serving the stale tool list forever. The snapshot was always
   documented as a parent-lookup-failed fallback — make that explicit
   by reading `state.agent_service.get_agent_async` at cold-start and
   falling back to the stored snapshot only if the lookup fails.

2. `update_agent` trusted `agent_from_network(&net_agent).permissions`
   for the PUT response's permissions projection. When aura-network
   accepts the write but omits `permissions` from its response body
   (or an older deployment drops the field on serialize), the response
   deserializes to `AgentPermissions::default()` and `patchAgent` in
   the UI wipes every toggle the user just saved. After a successful
   PUT, compare the response's permissions to what we submitted and —
   on mismatch — trust the request-side value while logging an
   upstream regression warning.

Together this makes "toggle ReadAgent on → Save → send message →
`list_agents` is available" work for regular agents in project
contexts without any backend changes.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: read fresh parent caps per instance chat turn, guard PUT echo

rawCommits.subject: refactor(update-control): share UpdateControl across settings surfaces
rawCommits.body:
Move the update status/upgrade control into components/UpdateControl so
it can be rendered from both the standalone Settings view and the Team
Settings > General panel, which is the one most users actually see. This
adds the 'latest version'/upgrade UI next to the existing Version row in
the Team Settings About group.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: share UpdateControl across settings surfaces

rawCommits.subject: perf(chat): fan out per-session event reads and bound by target window
rawCommits.body:
Replace the sequential per-session list_events loop in
load_latest_agent_events_from_storage_result and
load_project_session_history with a shared helper that walks sessions
newest-first, fetches events in parallel batches of four, and stops as
soon as the caller's limit+offset window is satisfied. Historical chat
opens with many past sessions no longer pay O(total lifetime events) of
storage reads on every open.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(chat): fan out per-session event reads and bound by target window

rawCommits.subject: style(org-settings): move Updates row to the end of the About group
rawCommits.body:
Reorder the About group to Version / Commit / Built / Updates so the
interactive upgrade control sits at the bottom of the static build
metadata instead of splitting the Version row from Commit and Built.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(org-settings): move Updates row to the end of the About group

rawCommits.subject: style(update-control): wrap and right-align the server-managed message
rawCommits.body:
Give the unsupported-browser message its own class that caps the width,
right-aligns the text, and removes the unused top margin so it reads as
two balanced lines at the end of the row instead of a single long line.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(update-control): wrap and right-align the server-managed message

rawCommits.subject: perf(chat): cache project-agent discovery with a short TTL
rawCommits.body:
Wrap `find_matching_project_agents` with a 30s in-memory cache keyed by
(jwt, agent_id). The orgs -> projects -> project_agents fan-out
underneath it was the dominant fixed cost on every chat open and every
chat turn, and since bindings change only on explicit create/delete
paths a <=30s stale result is safe. On cold boot the active agent's
history fetch populates the cache, and the sidebar preview prefetches
for other agents then hit it instead of re-walking the same
orgs/projects tree.

Invalidate on the lazy Home-project auto-bind path so the re-read after
`ensure_agent_home_project_and_binding` sees the new binding rather
than the empty snapshot the first call populated.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(chat): cache project-agent discovery with a short TTL

rawCommits.subject: perf(agents): deprioritize sidebar preview prefetch on cold boot
rawCommits.body:
The agents sidebar was fetching a 80-event history preview for every
other agent as soon as the list loaded, with concurrency 4. On cold
boot this collided with the foreground chat's own history fetch: the
active agent's request was queued behind N parallel previews and
raced against them for backend capacity, pushing first-paint out.

Three tweaks, no behavior change once everything settles:

- Exclude the currently selected agent from the prefetch queue. The
  active chat view is already fetching that one; running it here just
  puts the foreground request at the back of the line.
- Wait for the active chat's history to resolve before kicking off
  previews, so the foreground round-trip has an uncontested path.
- Drop concurrency from 4 to 2 and schedule via `requestIdleCallback`
  so the previews fill in during browser idle time rather than
  competing with initial render work.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(agents): deprioritize sidebar preview prefetch on cold boot

rawCommits.subject: perf(chat): persist chat history to IndexedDB for instant paint on reload
rawCommits.body:
`useChatHistoryStore` was memory-only, so every browser reload
re-started every chat view at status: "loading" and flashed a spinner
until the server round-trip resolved. Add a `chatHistory` object store
to the shared `aura-browser-store` IDB wrapper and:

- Write the final display-event list there whenever `fetchHistory`
  succeeds.
- Expose `hydrateFromCache(key)` that populates the in-memory entry
  from IDB when the caller mounts with no entry yet. Uses the
  persisted timestamp (not `fetchedAt: 0`) so `useChatHistorySync`'s
  stale-guard doesn't treat it as loading and the view paints
  immediately while the network revalidation runs in parallel.
- Call `hydrateFromCache` from `useChatHistorySync` alongside
  `fetchHistory`, and clear the cached entry on explicit
  `clearHistory`.

IDB failures fall back to the existing localStorage shim via
`browserDbSet`/`browserDbGet`, so the feature degrades gracefully in
private browsing contexts where IDB is blocked.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(chat): persist chat history to IndexedDB for instant paint on reload

rawCommits.subject: perf(agents): drop the /agents index lazy-load + agent-list gate
rawCommits.body:
Two cold-boot waterfall steps on first navigation to `/agents`:

1. `AgentChatView` was `React.lazy`-imported from the agents route
   table, adding a dynamic import round-trip to the critical path.
   The projects app already imports `AgentChatView` eagerly (see
   `apps/projects/routes.tsx`), so splitting it here saved zero bytes
   in the initial bundle — just cost a chunk fetch. Import eagerly.

2. `AgentIndexRedirect` waited for `fetchAgents` to resolve before
   deciding where to go, even when `localStorage.lastAgentId` already
   told us exactly which agent the user wants. Desktop now redirects
   synchronously off the cached id; `AgentChatView` will handle any
   stale id via its existing missing-agent flow, so no correctness
   regression. Mobile still shows the library prompt.

Combined, first meaningful paint on `/agents` no longer blocks on
either the chunk fetch or the agents-list network round-trip.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(agents): drop the /agents index lazy-load + agent-list gate

rawCommits.subject: fix(permissions): preserve shadow bundle when aura-network drops the permissions column on read
rawCommits.body:
The PUT path already had defensive reconciliation that trusts the
caller's submitted bundle when aura-network fails to echo it back,
but the GET path blindly overwrote the local shadow with whatever
`permissions` column the network response carried. For non-CEO
library agents that column comes back empty, so every `GET /agents`
on app boot clobbered the toggles the user had saved — capabilities
appeared to stick in the current session and then silently turn off
on the next restart. CEO agents were immune only because
`normalized_for_identity` re-expands their bundle to the canonical
preset after the network hydrate.

Adds a symmetric read-side reconciliation helper on `AgentService`
and wires it into every path that turns a `NetworkAgent` into an
`Agent` before saving the shadow: `list_agents`, `get_agent`,
`hydrate_shadow_from_network`, `get_agent_async`,
`get_agent_with_jwt`, and `installed_tools::load_agent`. Also aligns
`SessionConfig.agent_permissions` with the already-normalized
bundle `installed_tools` is built from so the harness dispatcher's
capability checks can't disagree with the surfaced toolset.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: preserve shadow bundle when aura-network drops the permissions column on read

rawCommits.subject: feat(permissions): autosave capability toggles on change, replace Save/Discard bar with status pill
rawCommits.body:
Toggling a capability, scope chip, or project-access entry in the
Permissions sidekick tab now persists automatically ~350ms after
the last edit — no more Save/Discard bar to remember. Rapid flicker
coalesces into a single PUT via a debounce timer, and if the tab
unmounts or the user switches agents with a pending debounce, the
latest draft is flushed synchronously on teardown so nothing gets
silently dropped.

A small `AutosaveStatus` pill in the Scope section header echoes
the save lifecycle: spinner + "Saving…" while in flight, a brief
"Saved" flash on success, and a red "Save failed" label with an
inline Retry action on error. Keyed the prop-sync effect on
`agent_id` only (not `agent.permissions`) so the `patchAgent`
round-trip after our own successful save can't race against
in-flight keystrokes and snap the draft back to a stale snapshot.

Also fills in the `readAllProjects` / `writeAllProjects` entries
in `CAPABILITY_LABELS` that had fallen out of sync with the wire
type (the build started failing once the test suite referenced
them via the corrected CEO fixture).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: autosave capability toggles on change, replace Save/Discard bar with status pill

rawCommits.subject: ci(android): fail fast on missing fastlane release secrets
rawCommits.body:
The `ship-android` job was dying deep inside `fastlane android release` with
`Missing required environment variable ANDROID_PLAY_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON_BASE64`,
and only ever surfaced the first missing var at a time. Add a preflight that
checks every required secret for the selected lane in one pass and prints an
actionable error before the fastlane step runs. Wire it into both
android-mobile.yml (ship-android) and release-mobile-nightly.yml (build-android,
github_release lane) so signed-build and Play Store paths both fail loudly and
early when a secret is unset.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: fail fast on missing fastlane release secrets

rawCommits.cleanSubject: retry transient packaging failures

rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add Kimi K2.6 to Aura chat models

rawCommits.cleanSubject: desktop: stage updater sidecar outside install tree

rendered.title: Agent permissions overhaul, in-app upgrades, and nightly pipeline hardening
rendered.intro: A dense nightly that reworks how agent capabilities flow from UI toggles to live tool sessions, adds a proper in-app update control for desktop users, and shores up the nightly release pipeline across Android, macOS packaging, version numbering, and the updater sidecar. Chat opens also get noticeably faster on cold boot and reload.

entries.title: Agent capabilities, skills, and chat startup get a major rework
entries.summary: A broad late-night push unified the agent tool registry, fixed long-standing permission toggle bugs, added an in-app upgrade control, and cut cold-boot latency for chat.

items.text: Rebuilt the agent tool registry around a single capability-aware pipeline: every tool now declares an Always/OnDemand surface, CEO and non-CEO bundles flow through the same permission filter, and saving a capability change invalidates live chat sessions (direct and project-instance) plus the dispatcher permissions cache so toggles take effect on the next turn without a server restart. Also restored missing CEO tools like list_agents, send_to_agent, and list_agent_instances.

Closed three separate paths where permission edits silently reverted: project-instance chats now read fresh parent capabilities at cold-start instead of a stale snapshot, update_agent trusts the submitted bundle when aura-network omits permissions in its PUT echo, and GET /agents no longer clobbers the local shadow when the network response drops the permissions column.

Replaced the Save/Discard bar in the Permissions sidekick with debounced autosave and an inline status pill (Saving… / Saved / Save failed + Retry), and restyled the capability toggles with a high-contrast track so on/off state reads clearly against the dark panel.

Hardened the Delete skill flow: the server now refuses with 409 when a user-authored skill is still installed on any agent and returns the blocking {agent_id, name} list for inline display in the confirm modal, and the catalog proxy hides entries whose SKILL.md no longer exists on disk so deleted skills stop reappearing under Available.

Shipped an in-app upgrade control on desktop: a new block under Settings > Version (and mirrored into Team Settings > General > About) surfaces latest-version state, check-for-updates, install, and download/install/failure states, with a 'managed by the server' fallback on the web.

Cut cold-boot latency for chat: history now hydrates instantly from an IndexedDB cache while the network revalidates, the /agents index skips a lazy chunk fetch and agent-list gate by redirecting off the cached last agent id, and the sidebar preview prefetch yields to the foreground chat via requestIdleCallback with lower concurrency.

Reduced server-side chat open cost: per-session event reads now fan out in parallel batches and stop as soon as the limit+offset window is filled, and the orgs→projects→project_agents discovery behind every chat turn is cached for 30s per (jwt, agent_id) with targeted invalidation on auto-bind.

entries.title: Android release jobs preflight their signing secrets
entries.summary: The ship-android and nightly mobile jobs now fail fast with an actionable error when Play Store or signing secrets are missing, instead of dying deep inside fastlane.

items.text: Added a preflight that checks every required secret for the selected lane in one pass and wired it into both android-mobile.yml and release-mobile-nightly.yml, so signed-build and Play Store paths surface all missing variables up front rather than one-at-a-time fastlane errors.

entries.title: Transient macOS and Linux packaging failures now auto-retry
entries.summary: Desktop packaging across nightly, stable, and validate workflows retries known transient failures up to three times, with stale DMG mounts cleaned up between attempts.

items.text: Replaced the single-shot retry with a three-attempt loop across desktop-validate, release-nightly, and release-stable that matches hdiutil 'Resource busy' on macOS and 429/5xx AppRun download errors on Linux, and extracted the stale-DMG cleanup into a shared script that runs between retries.

entries.title: Kimi K2.6 joins the chat model picker
entries.summary: The chat input bar's model list now includes Kimi K2.6 alongside the existing Claude, GPT, and Codex options.

items.text: Added Kimi K2.6 to the default model constants and updated ChatInputBar so it shows up in the quick picker; the bundled model ids were also normalized to the aura-* prefix used throughout the app.

entries.title: Nightly version numbers stay stable across rerun attempts
entries.summary: Nightly build, package, and Android jobs now share a single resolved version so rerunning a failed attempt no longer produces mismatched artifact versions.

items.text: Hoisted nightly version resolution into a dedicated resolve-version job consumed by build-app, package, and package-android-apk, dropping the per-job run_attempt suffix that caused reruns to mint new versions, and added a validate-release-artifact-versions script to catch drift between produced artifacts.

entries.title: Desktop updater stages its sidecar outside the install tree
entries.summary: The desktop app now copies the bundled harness binary into a versioned staging directory under the user data folder before launching it, and routes install-update requests through the main event loop.

items.text: Bundled harness binaries are staged into <data_dir>/runtime/sidecar under a content-addressed name derived from size, mtime, and package version, so the updater can replace the install tree without the running sidecar holding files open. The /update/install HTTP route now dispatches a UserEvent::InstallUpdate through the event loop proxy instead of calling into the updater inline.

highlights: Unified, autosaving agent permissions that take effect without a restart
In-app upgrade control in Settings and Team Settings
Faster cold chat opens with IDB-cached history and cached project discovery
Kimi K2.6 available in the chat model picker
More resilient nightly packaging, versioning, and desktop updater staging
